sourceMovie.title: Survivor
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Survivor (2015) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. James McTeigue's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Salt
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Survivor clicked for you, Salt (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Taking of Pelham 1 2 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Survivor clicked for you, The Taking of Pelham 1 2 3 (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Survivor?
faq.answer: Try Salt, The Taking of Pelham 1 2 3, RocknRolla—each shares DNA with Survivor while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Survivor?
faq.answer: Survivor (2015) is best described as Action, Crime, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Survivor worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
